snippet.title: BNY Mellon CEO Vince Gives Fed `An A-Grade' on Economy
snippet.description:
Bank of New York Mellon Corp. Chief Executive Officer Robin Vince told Bloomberg's Romaine Bostick that he is ``rooting for the fact that we'll have a soft landing'' and praised the Federal Reserve for its policy responses. ``The US economy has performed remarkably well,'' Vince said.                        


Follow Bloomberg for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more: http://www.bloomberg.com

snippet.title: BNY Mellon CEO: I am 'struck by' to see how interested European CEOs are at investing in the U.S.
snippet.description:

snippet.title: BNY Mellon CEO Expects New Capital Proposals to 'Evolve'
snippet.description:
Bank of New York Mellon Corp. Chief Executive Officer Robin Vince says he expects proposals for stricter capital requirements to evolve over time. He also speaks to Bloomberg's Romaine Bostick about the stability of the financial sector, Federal Reserve monetary policy and the bank's 240 year history.

snippet.title: Rewiring the Globe for Resilience | Davos 2023 | World Economic Forum
snippet.description:
In the face of continuous cycles of disruption, countries and companies have started to devise their own resilience plans, with investment in infrastructure emerging as essential to drive both the economic and climate agendas.

How can these actors invest and work together to build sustainable and inclusive economic growth?

This session is directly linked to the work of the Resilience Consortium of the World Economic Forum.

Speakers: Børge Brende, Bob Sternfels, Khaldoon Al Mubarak, Mariya Gabriel, Robin Vince, Rania Al-Mashat

00:00 Introduction
02:21 Discussion begins


The World Economic Forum is the International Organization for Public-Private Cooperation. The Forum engages the foremost political, business, cultural and other leaders of society to shape global, regional and industry agendas. We believe that progress happens by bringing together people from all walks of life who have the drive and the influence to make positive change.

World Economic Forum Website ► http://www.weforum.org/
Facebook ► https://www.facebook.com/worldeconomicforum/
YouTube ► https://www.youtube.com/wef
Instagram ► https://www.instagram.com/worldeconomicforum/ 
Twitter ► https://twitter.com/wef
LinkedIn ► https://www.linkedin.com/company/world-economic-forum
TikTok ► https://www.tiktok.com/@worldeconomicforum
Flipboard ► https://flipboard.com/@WEF

#Davos #WEF23 #WorldEconomicForum

snippet.title: On This Day: November 3, 1988 - The Infamous Brawl on Geraldo Rivera's Talk Show
snippet.description:
On November 3, 1988, a television taping took a violent turn when Geraldo Rivera's talk show, focusing on 'Teen Hatemongers', erupted into a brawl. The incident, which resulted in Rivera's nose being broken, was sparked by racial tensions and confrontations between the guests. Dive into the events of that tumultuous day and the impact it had on talk show history.

Make sure to LIKE, SUBSCRIBE, and hit that NOTIFICATION bell for more exciting historical events! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC-cRbG2RlFzKjY-jME4Oy3g

snippet.title: PRIME Roger Federer 💫
snippet.description:
Roger Federer in his prime as he pulls off an incredible tweener against Novak Djokovic in the semifinals of the 2009 US Open. #Shorts

Don't miss a moment of the US Open! Subscribe now: https://bit.ly/2Pdr81i

The US Open is the final Grand Slam of the year and takes place at the USTA Billie Jean King National Tennis Center. The 2022 US Open saw Spain's Carlos Alcaraz and Poland's Iga Swiatek claim the men's and women's singles titles. It was both players' first US Open title, with Alcaraz claiming his first Grand Slam title and Swiatek claiming her third Grand Slam title. The 2023 US Open main draw is scheduled to go ahead between August 28 - September 10. Look out for highlights, extended highlights, full matches, press conferences, on-court interviews, hot shots, memorable moments, compilations and more!

localized.description:
Roger Federer in his prime as he pulls off an incredible tweener against Novak Djokovic in the semifinals of the 2009 US Open. #Shorts

Don't miss a moment of the US Open! Subscribe now: https://bit.ly/2Pdr81i

The US Open is the final Grand Slam of the year and takes place at the USTA Billie Jean King National Tennis Center. The 2022 US Open saw Spain's Carlos Alcaraz and Poland's Iga Swiatek claim the men's and women's singles titles. It was both players' first US Open title, with Alcaraz claiming his first Grand Slam title and Swiatek claiming her third Grand Slam title. The 2023 US Open main draw is scheduled to go ahead between August 28 - September 10.
